title: Authenticated users can steer regular web-push reminder delivery to arbitrary stored endpoints

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: web_push_subscriptions stores user_id, endpoint, p256dh, auth, and timezone as database fields.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: owner_all policy allows an authenticated user to insert/update rows for their own user_id without endpoint host/scheme validation or proof that endpoint came from PushManager.

affected_locations.label: closest_control
affected_locations.detail: Normal client path uses PushManager then upserts the row, but this is client-side provenance only and does not constrain direct Supabase API writes.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Cron-secret-gated service-role function reads all stored web_push_subscriptions endpoints.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Stored endpoint and keys are passed into webPush.sendNotification.

attacker_controlled_source: An authenticated user with a Supabase session can write their own web_push_subscriptions row via the Data API, setting endpoint, p256dh, auth, and timezone while satisfying user_id = auth.uid().
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The service-role cron job later trusts endpoint as a Web Push destination and passes it to webPush.sendNotification without a server-side allowlist or endpoint provenance check.
impact: Potential SSRF/callback abuse from a scheduled service-role Edge Function toward attacker-selected HTTPS endpoints, including internal or cloud/LAN destinations if the runtime/network and web-push URL handling permit them; also leaks push request metadata/VAPID authorization material to attacker-controlled endpoints.
why_plausible: Repository evidence shows the database allows owner-managed endpoint writes and the sender reads the stored endpoint directly. The only PushManager proof exists in client code and is bypassable by direct authenticated API calls.
closest_apparent_control: Cron secret prevents direct invocation of the sender, and the client normally writes browser-generated endpoints; neither prevents a legitimate authenticated user from planting a row consumed by the next scheduled run.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Source/control/sink traced across migration policy, client provenance-only control, and service-role sender. Runtime proof of web-push accepted URL schemes/hosts and Supabase Edge egress reachability remains for centralized validation.

candidate_local_attack_path.entry: Supabase Data API insert/update to web_push_subscriptions for own user_id
candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: user-controlled stored endpoint crosses into service-role cron network client
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Attacker has an account; a due reminder exists or can be configured for the attacker's account; scheduled web-push-reminders runs with VAPID configured.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Sender requires x-cron-secret and normal UI uses PushManager, but neither is dispositive for stored endpoint provenance.

title: Authenticated users can steer proactive coach push delivery to arbitrary stored endpoints

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: web_push_subscriptions stores user-controlled endpoint and key material.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Current hardened owner_all policy still permits authenticated users to manage their own subscription rows without destination allowlist or browser-origin proof.

affected_locations.label: closest_control
affected_locations.detail: Normal client path uses PushManager, but the server-side table policy does not require this provenance.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Cron-secret-gated service-role function reads all stored subscription endpoints when coach_push is enabled.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Stored endpoint and keys are passed into webPush.sendNotification.

attacker_controlled_source: An authenticated user can plant or alter their own web_push_subscriptions endpoint through Supabase Data API under the owner_all policy.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The proactive coach cron job consumes the stored endpoint as an outbound Web Push destination with no server-side host/scheme allowlist or endpoint provenance check.
impact: Potential SSRF/callback abuse from an independent service-role cron path when coach_push is enabled, with possible internal/network reachability and exposure of push request metadata to attacker-selected destinations.
why_plausible: The coach-push function is a separate scheduled sender that reads the same database-controlled endpoint and calls webPush.sendNotification independently from the regular reminder sender.
closest_apparent_control: The function requires COACH_PUSH_CRON_SECRET and a feature flag, but those only gate job execution. They do not constrain endpoints already stored by authenticated users.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Source/control/sink traced across hardened RLS policy, provenance-only client control, and coach-push sender. Runtime proof of feature enablement, URL handling, and egress reachability remains for centralized validation.

candidate_local_attack_path.entry: Supabase Data API write to own web_push_subscriptions row
candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: stored user-controlled endpoint crosses into service-role proactive coach sender
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Attacker has an account and push row; coach_push feature flag and VAPID secrets are enabled; a coach signal becomes eligible for the user.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Feature flag defaults off in migration and cron secret protects invocation; if disabled in production this instance is not reachable, but repository code ships the enabled path.
